name: SoundFile (Audio File I/O via libsndfile)

auth.notes: Local library; wraps libsndfile C library

performance.notes: C libsndfile backend; fast for WAV/FLAC/OGG; large files streamed

agent_readiness.notes: Read/write audio files via libsndfile. import soundfile as sf. data, samplerate = sf.read('file.wav'). data is float64 numpy array. sf.write('out.wav', data, samplerate). Formats: WAV, FLAC, OGG, AIFF, AU. Streaming: with sf.SoundFile('file.wav') as f: while True: data = f.read(1024); if not len(data): break. f.samplerate, f.channels, f.frames, f.subtype. sf.info('file.wav') → SoundFileInfo. No MP3 support (use librosa+audioread or pydub+ffmpeg). scipy.io.wavfile alternative for WAV-only.

gotchas: No MP3 support — libsndfile doesn't include MP3 patent-encumbered codec
Returns float64 by default — use dtype='int16' for 16-bit PCM
Multichannel audio returns shape (frames, channels) — not (channels, frames)
No audio effects or synthesis — just I/O (use librosa or pedalboard for processing)
best_when: Reading and writing WAV, FLAC, OGG audio files
Streaming large audio files in chunks without loading into memory
Getting sample rate and metadata from audio files
When a simple, fast audio file I/O library is needed without MP3 requirements
avoid_when: MP3 files (use librosa with audioread, or pydub with ffmpeg)
Real-time audio I/O (use sounddevice or pyaudio)
summary: SoundFile reads/writes audio via libsndfile. sf.read('file.wav') → (numpy_array, samplerate). sf.write('out.wav', data, sr). WAV, FLAC, OGG, AIFF formats. Streaming: SoundFile context manager with .read(1024). sf.info() for metadata. BSD-3. Fast, minimal dependency audio I/O. No MP3 (no patent codec). Returns float64 by default. Shape is (frames, channels). For MP3 use librosa or pydub+ffmpeg.
